raw.objective: “…to determine Vm and Km of PHT using F-PHT serum concentration data from routine monitoring of adult out-patients receiving PHT monotherapy for the management of epilepsy.”
raw.covariates: Tested: AGE, SEX, WT Included: none
raw.observations: No covariates were found, which may limit the applicability of the model, which based on the goodness-of-fit seems to describe the data well. Indeed, the data showed fairly low variability, which may have been the reason for not finding any significant covariates. A small pilot test group (N=7) was used to validate the model, which showed promising results
raw.equations: Vmax=theta1 Km=theta2* (1+eta1) D = ((Vmax*Css)/(Km*Css)) * (1+eps1) D is the daily dosage of phenytoin (mg/day), this formula can be rearranged to provide the Css based on dose and PK parameters: Css=(Km*D)/(Vmax-D)
raw.validation: No / Yes (validation by predicting Css for 7 patients)

omega.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

sigma.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale SIGMA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

extraction_target.residual_error: Vmax=theta1 Km=theta2* (1+eta1) D = ((Vmax*Css)/(Km*Css)) * (1+eps1) D is the daily dosage of phenytoin (mg/day), this formula can be rearranged to provide the Css based on dose and PK parameters: Css=(Km*D)/(Vmax-D)
extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.objective: “…to determine Vm and Km of PHT using F-PHT serum concentration data from routine monitoring of adult out-patients receiving PHT monotherapy for the management of epilepsy.”

covariates.raw: Tested: AGE, SEX, WT Included: none

validation.raw: No / Yes (validation by predicting Css for 7 patients)

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
